report_name: 26 Malicious 'FakeWallet' Crypto Apps Found on Apple App Store, Stealing Seed Phrases

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: The malicious FakeWallet apps used icons, names, and descriptions nearly identical to their legitimate cryptocurrency wallet counterparts to deceive users into downloading and trusting them.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The attackers successfully published and delivered 26 malicious applications via Apple's official App Store, bypassing its security review process to reach users.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The FakeWallet apps presented a fake wallet import interface to capture and steal the user's secret recovery phrase (seed phrase), which functions as the primary access token for a cryptocurrency wallet.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The campaign used social engineering tactics, exploiting user trust in the App Store and the appearance of legitimate apps to trick them into providing their sensitive seed phrases.

malware.name: FakeWallet
malware.description: A family of 26 malicious iOS applications that impersonate popular cryptocurrency wallets to steal users' recovery phrases, seed phrases, and private keys, which are then exfiltrated to attacker-controlled servers.

campaigns.name: FakeWallet Campaign
campaigns.description: A coordinated malware campaign active since at least fall 2025, involving 26 fraudulent cryptocurrency wallet applications on Apple's App Store designed to steal user seed phrases and private keys.
campaigns.objective: Steal cryptocurrency assets by tricking users into revealing their wallet recovery phrases.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: Operator of the official App Store where the 26 malicious FakeWallet apps were discovered and subsequently removed.

identities.name: Cryptocurrency Users
identities.description: The primary targets of the FakeWallet campaign, at risk of having their cryptocurrency assets stolen after entering their seed phrases into the malicious apps.

identities.name: MetaMask
identities.description: A popular cryptocurrency wallet whose branding and user interface were impersonated by the FakeWallet malware.

identities.name: Coinbase
identities.description: A popular cryptocurrency exchange and wallet provider whose branding and user interface were impersonated by the FakeWallet malware.

identities.name: Trust Wallet
identities.description: A popular cryptocurrency wallet whose branding and user interface were impersonated by the FakeWallet malware.

identities.name: Bitpie
identities.description: A cryptocurrency wallet whose branding and user interface were impersonated by the FakeWallet malware.

identities.name: TokenPocket
identities.description: A cryptocurrency wallet whose branding and user interface were impersonated by the FakeWallet malware.

identities.name: Ledger
identities.description: A hardware wallet manufacturer whose brand was impersonated by the FakeWallet malware.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Exfiltration Servers
infrastructure.description: Servers used by the FakeWallet operators to receive stolen seed phrases and private keys from victims' devices.

infrastructure.name: Trojanized Wallet Distribution Sites
infrastructure.description: Malicious web pages to which some fake apps redirected users to distribute trojanized versions of wallet software, outside of the App Store.

indicators.name: Potential Cryptocurrency Seed Phrase Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Cryptocurrency Seed Phrase Exfiltration
id: 2a9b4c1d-8e7f-4b1a-9c8d-6f5e4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network traffic containing patterns consistent with 12- or 24-word cryptocurrency seed phrases, which could indicate theft by malware like FakeWallet.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/26-fakewallet-apps-found-on-apple-app.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - attack.t1635
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate wallet recovery processes, although these are rare and should be investigated. The rule may need tuning to reduce noise from other sources of high-entropy text.
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|re: '(\b[a-z]+\b[\s\-.,_]+){11,23}\b[a-z]+\b'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic containing patterns consistent with cryptocurrency seed phrases (e.g., 12 or 24 dictionary words), indicating potential theft by malware like FakeWallet.
